annotations.note: The main existence theorem. Mathlib's statement gives a C¹ solution α on [tmin, tmax] with α(t₀) = x and α'(t) = f(t, α(t)).
Continuity of f near (t₀, y₀) is one field of the IsPicardLindelof hypothesis bundle (`continuousOn`); this exact ε-δ decomposition is internal to the proof, not a standalone decl.
The cylinder C_{a,b} corresponds to Mathlib's hypothesis parameters `a` (radius around x₀) and the time interval `Icc tmin tmax` — packaged inside IsPicardLindelof but not given its own name.
An explicit interval-decomposition; no dedicated decl. Mathlib uses `Set.Icc` and `Metric.closedBall` directly inside the proof without naming these projections.
The sup-norm on continuous functions exists in Mathlib via `BoundedContinuousFunction.norm`; the article's restriction to C(I_a, B_b) is implicit inside the Picard-Lindelöf proof, not its own decl.
The signature of the Picard operator. Mathlib's `ODE.picard` is defined as a function ℝ → E rather than as an endomorphism of a function space, but the role is identical.
The Picard iteration map. Mathlib defines this exact integral as `ODE.picard f t₀ x₀ t = x₀ + ∫ τ in t₀..t, f τ (φ τ)`.
An invariant on the iteration domain; proof step, not its own decl.
Proof step showing Γ maps the iteration domain to itself. Internal to Mathlib's proof of `IsPicardLindelof.exists_eq_forall_mem_Icc_hasDerivWithinAt`.
Banach-contraction estimate for Γ. Internal to Mathlib's proof; relies on the Lipschitz hypothesis `IsPicardLindelof.lipschitzOnWith`.
The fixed-point equation Γφ = φ. Mathlib's variant states that the solution φ equals (an iteration of) Γφ on the interval — capturing the same Banach-fixed-point fact.

The inductive Γᵐ contraction estimate (factorial decay). Mathlib's proof iterates the contraction internally and uses Banach's fixed-point theorem on a complete metric space; this exact factorial bound is not a standalone decl.
Choosing m large enough that Γᵐ becomes a contraction on an arbitrary subinterval. Implicit inside Mathlib's interval-optimisation step.

Lemma stating Γᵐ contraction with factorial bound L^m|t−t₀|^m/m! · ‖φ₁−φ₂‖ on the closed interval [t₀−α, t₀+α]. Not a standalone Mathlib decl — the proof of `IsPicardLindelof.exists_eq_forall_mem_Icc_hasDerivWithinAt` applies Banach's fixed-point theorem to the m-th iterate of `ODE.picard` internally, without naming this intermediate bound.
